idea: Build ResilienceRoom, an enterprise continuity simulator connecting fictional disruptions, capability dependencies, mitigation choices, recovery metrics, authorization thresholds, and a downloadable local evidence record.
audience: Enterprise resilience leads running a continuity planning exercise
outcome: Leads can compare mitigations and authorize a recovery plan only when transparent deterministic thresholds are met.
constraints: Local-only with deterministic fictional data and no backend, auth, database, analytics, APIs, or runtime network calls
Document all metric formulas, thresholds, units, and rounding
Generate the evidence export entirely in the browser and provide reset
Responsive and keyboard accessible with accessible dependency and metric representations
must_have_capabilities: Choose a disruption and show affected capability dependencies
Compare three mitigation choices with deterministic before-and-after metrics
Block authorization when recovery thresholds are not met and explain each failure
Authorize a sufficient plan and record the decision state
Download a deterministic local evidence record and reset the simulation
visual_direction: A judge-ready resilience command room balancing dependency clarity, explainable metrics, and confident enterprise visual polish.
demo_preferences: Choose a disruption, inspect affected capabilities, simulate an insufficient mitigation and show authorization blocked, choose a sufficient mitigation, verify thresholds, authorize, export evidence, then reset.
